info.title: enterprise_ai_backend
info.description: Enterprise AI Reliability Platform - Backend API (full build)

post.description:
Weighted composite reliability score across multiple signals.

Each component has a ``value`` in [0, 1] and a ``weight``. Components
are combined into a single 0-100 composite score, placed into a tier
(LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH), and optionally rolled up per NIST AI RMF function.

Each call is persisted (Sprint 2 E2-S3) so
``GET /reliability/score/history`` can trend it over time.

Composite reliability score + per-component explanation.

Extends ``POST /reliability/score`` with an ``explanation`` object that
identifies:

* ``top_driver`` and ``top_gap`` components,
* per-component contributions (absolute and percent of the composite),
* distance to adjacent tier boundaries (``tier_gap``),
* weakest / strongest NIST AI RMF function, and
* a one-sentence plain-English recommendation.

Also persists the underlying score record (Sprint 2 E2-S3).

get.description:
Return recent composite scores (newest first) plus trend stats.

Populated automatically by every ``POST /reliability/score`` and
``POST /reliability/score/explain`` call. The ``stats`` object
includes rolling average, min/max, a simple improving / degrading /
stable trend classification, and any tier transitions.

parameters.name: system_name

schema.description: Filter by system name (exact match). Omit for all systems.
schema.title: System Name

parameters.description: Filter by system name (exact match). Omit for all systems.

post.description:
Evaluate the policy gate on a reliability score input.

The request body carries the same shape as ``POST /reliability/score``
(a ``system_name`` + list of ``ReliabilityScoreComponent``) plus an
optional ``thresholds`` override. The response is an ``allow`` /
``warn`` / ``block`` decision with a list of rationale ``reasons``,
the applied thresholds (echoed), and the resolved composite score
and tier.

Sprint 3, E3-S3: every call is persisted as a
:class:`database.PolicyEvaluationRecord` so
``GET /policy/history`` can return a trend over time without the
caller having to re-submit the inputs.

get.description:
Return the policy-evaluation audit log with trend statistics.

Records are newest-first. ``stats`` reports count, latest / earliest
decision + composite, per-decision counts and rates, rolling average
/ min / max composite, a ``trend_direction`` (improving / degrading
/ stable / insufficient_data) computed from composite scores, and a
chronological list of every decision change in the window.

records.title: Records
records.description: List of feature vectors (2+ records required)

gate_decision.description: Policy gate outcome computed at creation time (null for pre-E3-S2 rows).

gate_reasons.title: Gate Reasons
gate_reasons.description: Gate reasons that fired, worst-severity first.

AuditLogRecordOut.title: AuditLogRecordOut
AuditLogRecordOut.description: One persisted append-only audit ledger row.

AuditLogStats.title: AuditLogStats
AuditLogStats.description: Aggregate facts about the returned audit-history window.

ComplianceControlOut.title: ComplianceControlOut
ComplianceControlOut.description: One control row in the security/compliance evidence bundle.

ComplianceEvidenceBundleOut.title: ComplianceEvidenceBundleOut
ComplianceEvidenceBundleOut.description: Security/compliance evidence bundle assembled from repo-backed facts.

DashboardEpic.title: DashboardEpic
DashboardEpic.description: One backlog epic exposed to the dashboard.

DashboardMetric.title: DashboardMetric
DashboardMetric.description: Small KPI card used by the dashboard and executive export.

DashboardSummaryOut.title: DashboardSummaryOut
DashboardSummaryOut.description: Role-aware dashboard summary for the Sprint 4 web UI.

Epic.title: Epic
Epic.description: A product backlog epic and its current delivery status.

ExecutiveSummaryOut.title: ExecutiveSummaryOut
ExecutiveSummaryOut.description: Unified executive summary used by the JSON and PDF exports.

NISTBreakdown.title: NISTBreakdown
NISTBreakdown.description:
Per-function weighted-average score, 0-100. None if no components
in the request were tagged for that function.

composite_score.title: Composite Score
composite_score.description: Composite score at the moment of the transition.

PolicyDecisionTransition.title: PolicyDecisionTransition
PolicyDecisionTransition.description:
One decision change detected in the history stream.

Mirrors :class:`TierTransition` but for gate outcomes; shows when a
system crossed from one ``allow`` / ``warn`` / ``block`` state to
another.

reasons.title: Reasons
reasons.description: All rules that fired, worst severity first.

thresholds.description: Override the default thresholds; omit to use defaults.

code.title: Code
code.description: Short stable identifier for the rule.

PolicyReason.title: PolicyReason
PolicyReason.description: One machine-readable + human-readable rationale for the decision.

PolicySeverity.title: PolicySeverity
PolicySeverity.description: Severity classification for a single policy reason.

allow_min_composite.title: Allow Min Composite
allow_min_composite.description: Composite score >= this value maps to 'allow'.

warn_min_composite.title: Warn Min Composite
warn_min_composite.description: Composite score >= this (and < allow threshold) maps to 'warn'.

min_nist_function_score.title: Min Nist Function Score
min_nist_function_score.description: Per-function score floor; any tagged NIST function below this value forces 'block'.

PolicyThresholds.title: PolicyThresholds
PolicyThresholds.description:
Configurable thresholds controlling the gate outcome.

``allow_min_composite`` and ``warn_min_composite`` partition the
composite score into allow / warn / block bands; ``min_nist_function_score``
is a hard floor -- any per-NIST-function breakdown below it forces
``block`` regardless of composite.

rolling_average_composite.title: Rolling Average Composite
rolling_average_composite.description: Mean composite score across all returned records.

decision_transitions.title: Decision Transitions
decision_transitions.description: Every place the decision changed, chronological.

PolicyTrendStats.title: PolicyTrendStats
PolicyTrendStats.description: Aggregate statistics computed across a policy-history window.

weight.title: Weight
weight.description: Component weight; weights are normalized if they do not sum to 1.0.

nist_function.description: Optional NIST AI RMF function this component maps to.

ReliabilityScoreComponent.title: ReliabilityScoreComponent
ReliabilityScoreComponent.description: One input signal to the weighted reliability score.

weights_normalized.title: Weights Normalized
weights_normalized.description: True if input weights did not sum to 1.0 and were normalized.

weights_normalized.title: Weights Normalized
weights_normalized.description: True if input weights did not sum to 1.0 and were normalized.

ReliabilityScoreWithExplanation.title: ReliabilityScoreWithExplanation
ReliabilityScoreWithExplanation.description:
Response body for POST /reliability/score/explain.

Extends the base score response with a detailed ``explanation`` object
that tells the caller *why* the composite landed where it did and what
to do about it.

retention_days.title: Retention Days
retention_days.description: Days before an audit record becomes eligible for review. 0 is allowed for local validation.

contribution.title: Contribution
contribution.description: Component's share of the composite score on a 0-100 scale (normalized weight * value * 100).

contribution_percent.title: Contribution Percent
contribution_percent.description: Percent of the composite score attributable to this component (sums to 100 across all components when composite > 0).

ScoreContribution.title: ScoreContribution
ScoreContribution.description: One component's contribution to the composite score.

top_driver.description: Component contributing most to the composite score.

top_gap.description: Component with the lowest value (largest improvement opportunity).

recommendation.title: Recommendation
recommendation.description: One-sentence plain-English suggestion for the owner.

ScoreExplanation.title: ScoreExplanation
ScoreExplanation.description: Human- and machine-readable rationale for a composite score.

rolling_average.title: Rolling Average
rolling_average.description: Mean composite score across all returned records.

ScoreTrendStats.title: ScoreTrendStats
ScoreTrendStats.description: Aggregate statistics computed across a history window.

SprintSummary.title: SprintSummary
SprintSummary.description: High-level roadmap status for the frontend.

next_tier_up.title: Next Tier Up
next_tier_up.description: Tier label above the current one, or null if already at LOW.

points_needed_up.title: Points Needed Up
points_needed_up.description: Composite points needed to jump to the next tier up.

next_tier_down.title: Next Tier Down
next_tier_down.description: Tier label below the current one, or null if already at HIGH.

points_buffer_down.title: Points Buffer Down
points_buffer_down.description: Composite points of headroom before falling to the next tier down.

TierGap.title: TierGap
TierGap.description: How close the current composite is to adjacent tiers.

composite_score.title: Composite Score
composite_score.description: Composite score at the moment of the transition.

TierTransition.title: TierTransition
TierTransition.description: One tier change detected in the history stream.
